events.content:
Build an AI chat interface using Nuxt UI's chat components.

The page should display a conversation between a user and an assistant with the following requirements:

1. Use Nuxt UI's `ChatMessage` component to render individual messages
2. Each message must use the `parts` prop with the AI SDK message part format: `[{ type: 'text', text: '...' }]`
3. Messages must have a `role` prop set to either `"user"` or `"assistant"`
4. User messages should appear on the right side, assistant messages on the left
5. Use the `ChatMessages` component to wrap the list of messages
6. Include a `ChatPrompt` component at the bottom for user input
7. Include at least 2 hardcoded example messages (one user, one assistant) for demonstration

The chat does not need to be functional (no API calls required) — focus on correct component usage and props.

message.content:
Build an AI chat interface using Nuxt UI's chat components.

The page should display a conversation between a user and an assistant with the following requirements:

1. Use Nuxt UI's `ChatMessage` component to render individual messages
2. Each message must use the `parts` prop with the AI SDK message part format: `[{ type: 'text', text: '...' }]`
3. Messages must have a `role` prop set to either `"user"` or `"assistant"`
4. User messages should appear on the right side, assistant messages on the left
5. Use the `ChatMessages` component to wrap the list of messages
6. Include a `ChatPrompt` component at the bottom for user input
7. Include at least 2 hardcoded example messages (one user, one assistant) for demonstration

The chat does not need to be functional (no API calls required) — focus on correct component usage and props.

tool.result:
14217	# ChatMessage
14218	
14219	## Usage
14220	
14221	The ChatMessage component renders an `<article>` element for a `user` or `assistant` chat message.
14222	
14223	```vue
14224	<template>
14225	  <u-chat-message :avatar={"src":"https://github.com/benjamincanac.png","loading":"lazy"} :parts=[{"type":"text","id":"1","text":"Hello! Tell me more about building AI chatbots with Nuxt UI."}] id=1 role=user side=right variant=soft />
14226	</template>
14227	```
14228	
14229	\> \[!TIP]
14230	\> See: /docs/components/chat-messages
14231	\> Use the \`ChatMessages\` component to display a list of chat messages.
14232	
14233	### Parts
14234	
14235	Use the `parts` prop to display the message content using the AI SDK format.
14236	
14237	```vue
14238	<template>
14239	  <UChatMessage role="user" id="1" />
14240	</template>
14241	```
14242	
14243	\> \[!NOTE]
14244	\> The \`parts\` prop is the recommended format for the AI SDK. Each part has a \`type\` (e.g., 'text') and corresponding content. The ChatMessage component also supports the deprecated \`content\` prop for backward compatibility.
14245	
14246	### Side
14247	
14248	Use the `side` prop to display the message on the left or right.
14249	
14250	```vue
14251	<template>
14252	  <UChatMessage side="right" role="user" id="1" />
14253	</template>
14254	```
14255	
14256	\> \[!NOTE]
14257	\> When using the \[\`ChatMessages\`]\(/docs/components/chat-messages) component, the \`side\` prop is set to \`left\` for \`assistant\` messages and \`right\` for \`user\` messages.
14258	
14259	### Variant
14260	
14261	Use the `variant` prop to change style of the message.
14262	
14263	```vue
14264	<template>
14265	  <UChatMessage variant="soft" role="user" id="1" />
14266	</template>
14267	```
14268	
14269	\> \[!NOTE]
14270	\> When using the \[\`ChatMessages\`]\(/docs/components/chat-messages) component, the \`variant\` prop is set to \`naked\` for \`assistant\` messages and \`soft\` for \`user\` messages.
14271	
14272	### Icon
14273	
14274	Use the `icon` prop to display an [Icon](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/icon) component next to the message.
14275	
14276	```vue
14277	<template>
14278	  <UChatMessage icon="i-lucide-user" variant="soft" side="right" role="user" id="1" />
14279	</template>
14280	```
14281	
14282	### Avatar
14283	
14284	Use the `avatar` prop to display an [Avatar](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/avatar) component next to the message.
14285	
14286	```vue
14287	<template>
14288	  <UChatMessage variant="soft" side="right" role="user" id="1" />
14289	</template>
14290	```
14291	
14292	You can also use the `avatar.icon` prop to display an icon as the avatar.
14293	
14294	```vue
14295	<template>
14296	  <UChatMessage role="assistant" id="1" />
14297	</template>
14298	```
14299	
14300	### Actions
14301	
14302	Use the `actions` prop to display actions below the message that will be displayed when hovering over the message.
14303	
14304	```vue
14305	<script setup lang="ts">
14306	import type { ButtonProps } from '@nuxt/ui'
14307	</script>
14308	
14309	<template>
14310	  <UChatMessage role="user" id="1" />
14311	</template>
14312	```
14313	
14314	## Examples
14315	
14316	\> \[!TIP]
14317	\> See: /docs/components/chat
14318	\> Check the Chat overview page for installation instructions, server setup and usage examples.
14319	
14320	## API
14321	
14322	### Props
14323	
14324	```ts
14325	/**
14326	 * Props for the ChatMessage component
14327	 */
14328	interface ChatMessageProps {
14329	  /**
14330	   * A unique identifier for the message.
14331	   */
14332	  id: string;
14333	  /**
14334	   * The role of the message.
14335	   */
14336	  role: "system" | "user" | "assistant";
14337	  /**
14338	   * The parts of the message. Use this for rendering the message in the UI.
14339	   * 
14340	   * System messages should be avoided (set the system prompt on the server instead).
14341	   * They can have text parts.
14342	   * 
14343	   * User messages can have text parts and file parts.
14344	   * 
14345	   * Assistant messages can have text, reasoning, tool invocation, and file parts.
14346	   */
14347	  parts: UIMessagePart<UIDataTypes, UITools>[];
14348	  /**
14349	   * The element or component this component should render as.
14350	   * @default "\"article\""
14351	   */
14352	  as?: any;
14353	  icon?: any;
14354	  avatar?: (AvatarProps & { [key: string]: any; }) | undefined;
14355	  variant?: "solid" | "outline" | "soft" | "subtle" | "naked" | undefined;
14356	  side?: "left" | "right" | undefined;
14357	  /**
14358	   * Display a list of actions under the message.
14359	   * The `label` will be used in a tooltip.
14360	   * `{ size: 'xs', color: 'neutral', variant: 'ghost' }`{lang="ts-type"}
14361	   */
14362	  actions?: (Omit<ButtonProps, "onClick"> & { onClick?: ((e: MouseEvent, message: UIMessage<unknown, UIDataTypes, UITools>) => void) | undefined; })[] | undefined;
14363	  /**
14364	   * Render the message in a compact style.
14365	   * This is done automatically when used inside a `UChatPalette`{lang="ts-type"}.
14366	   */
14367	  compact?: boolean | undefined;
14368	  content?: string | undefined;
14369	  ui?: { root?: ClassNameValue; container?: ClassNameValue; leading?: ClassNameValue; leadingIcon?: ClassNameValue; leadingAvatar?: ClassNameValue; leadingAvatarSize?: ClassNameValue; files?: ClassNameValue; content?: ClassNameValue; actions?: ClassNameValue; } | undefined;
14370	  /**
14371	   * The metadata of the message.
14372	   */
14373	  metadata?: unknown;
14374	}
14375	```
14376	
14377	### Slots
14378	
14379	```ts
14380	/**
14381	 * Slots for the ChatMessage component
14382	 */
14383	interface ChatMessageSlots {
14384	  leading(): any;
14385	  files(): any;
14386	  content(): any;
14387	  actions(): any;
14388	}
14389	```
14390	
14391	## Theme
14392	
14393	```ts [app.config.ts]
14394	export default defineAppConfig({
14395	  ui: {
14396	    chatMessage: {
14397	      slots: {
14398	        root: 'group/message relative w-full',
14399	        container: 'relative flex items-start',
14400	        leading: 'inline-flex items-center justify-center min-h-6',
14401	        leadingIcon: 'shrink-0',
14402	        leadingAvatar: 'shrink-0',
14403	        leadingAvatarSize: '',
14404	        files: 'flex items-center gap-1.5 mb-1.5',
14405	        content: 'relative text-pretty min-w-0 *:first:mt-0 *:last:mb-0',
14406	        actions: [
14407	          'opacity-0 group-hover/message:opacity-100 absolute bottom-0 flex items-center',
14408	          'transition-opacity'
14409	        ]
14410	      },
14411	      variants: {
14412	        variant: {
14413	          solid: {
14414	            content: 'bg-inverted text-inverted'
14415	          },
14416	          outline: {

14360	   * `{ size: 'xs', color: 'neutral', variant: 'ghost' }`{lang="ts-type"}
14361	   */
14362	  actions?: (Omit<ButtonProps, "onClick"> & { onClick?: ((e: MouseEvent, message: UIMessage<unknown, UIDataTypes, UITools>) => void) | undefined; })[] | undefined;
14363	  /**
14364	   * Render the message in a compact style.
14365	   * This is done automatically when used inside a `UChatPalette`{lang="ts-type"}.
14366	   */
14367	  compact?: boolean | undefined;
14368	  content?: string | undefined;
14369	  ui?: { root?: ClassNameValue; container?: ClassNameValue; leading?: ClassNameValue; leadingIcon?: ClassNameValue; leadingAvatar?: ClassNameValue; leadingAvatarSize?: ClassNameValue; files?: ClassNameValue; content?: ClassNameValue; actions?: ClassNameValue; } | undefined;
14370	  /**
14371	   * The metadata of the message.
14372	   */
14373	  metadata?: unknown;
14374	}
14375	```
14376	
14377	### Slots
14378	
14379	```ts
14380	/**
14381	 * Slots for the ChatMessage component
14382	 */
14383	interface ChatMessageSlots {
14384	  leading(): any;
14385	  files(): any;
14386	  content(): any;
14387	  actions(): any;
14388	}
14389	```
14390	
14391	## Theme
14392	
14393	```ts [app.config.ts]
14394	export default defineAppConfig({
14395	  ui: {
14396	    chatMessage: {

14406	        actions: [
14407	          'opacity-0 group-hover/message:opacity-100 absolute bottom-0 flex items-center',
14408	          'transition-opacity'
14409	        ]
14410	      },
14411	      variants: {
14412	        variant: {
14413	          solid: {
14414	            content: 'bg-inverted text-inverted'
14415	          },
14416	          outline: {

tool.result:
14530	# ChatMessages
14531	
14532	## Usage
14533	
14534	The ChatMessages component displays a list of [ChatMessage](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/chat-message) components using either the default slot or the `messages` prop.
14535	
14536	```vue {2,8}
14537	<template>
14538	  <UChatMessages>
14539	    <UChatMessage
14540	      v-for="(message, index) in messages"
14541	      :key="index"
14542	      v-bind="message"
14543	    />
14544	  </UChatMessages>
14545	</template>
14546	```
14547	
14548	\> \[!NOTE]
14549	\> This component is purpose-built for AI chatbots with features like\:Initial scroll to the bottom upon loading (\[\`shouldScrollToBottom\`]\(#should-scroll-to-bottom)).Continuous scrolling down as new messages arrive (\[\`shouldAutoScroll\`]\(#should-auto-scroll)).An "Auto scroll" button appears when scrolled up, allowing users to jump back to the latest messages (\[\`autoScroll\`]\(#auto-scroll)).A loading indicator displays while the assistant is processing (\[\`status\`]\(#status)).Submitted messages are scrolled to the top of the viewport and the height of the last user message is dynamically adjusted.
14550	
14551	### Messages
14552	
14553	Use the `messages` prop to display a list of chat messages.
14554	
14555	```vue
14556	<template>
14557	  <UChatMessages />
14558	</template>
14559	```
14560	
14561	### Status
14562	
14563	Use the `status` prop to display a visual indicator when the assistant is processing.
14564	
14565	```vue
14566	<template>
14567	  <UChatMessages status="submitted" />
14568	</template>
14569	```
14570	
14571	\> \[!NOTE]
14572	\> Here's the detail of the different statuses from the AI SDK Chat class:\`submitted\`: The message has been sent to the API and we're awaiting the start of the response stream.\`streaming\`: The response is actively streaming in from the API, receiving chunks of data.\`ready\`: The full response has been received and processed; a new user message can be submitted.\`error\`: An error occurred during the API request, preventing successful completion.
14573	
14574	### User
14575	
14576	Use the `user` prop to change the [ChatMessage](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/chat-message) props for `user` messages. Defaults to:
14577	
14578	- `side: 'right'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14579	- `variant: 'soft'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14580	
14581	```vue
14582	<template>
14583	  <UChatMessages />
14584	</template>
14585	```
14586	
14587	### Assistant
14588	
14589	Use the `assistant` prop to change the [ChatMessage](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/chat-message) props for `assistant` messages. Defaults to:
14590	
14591	- `side: 'left'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14592	- `variant: 'naked'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14593	
14594	```vue
14595	<template>
14596	  <UChatMessages />
14597	</template>
14598	```
14599	
14600	### Auto Scroll
14601	
14602	Use the `auto-scroll` prop to customize or hide the auto scroll button (with `false` value) displayed when scrolling to the top of the chat. Defaults to:
14603	
14604	- `color: 'neutral'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14605	- `variant: 'outline'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14606	
14607	You can pass any property from the [Button](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/button) component to customize it.
14608	
14609	```vue
14610	<template>
14611	  <UChatMessages :should-scroll-to-bottom="false" />
14612	</template>
14613	```
14614	
14615	### Auto Scroll Icon
14616	
14617	Use the `auto-scroll-icon` prop to customize the auto scroll button [Icon](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/icon). Defaults to `i-lucide-arrow-down`.
14618	
14619	```vue
14620	<template>
14621	  <UChatMessages auto-scroll-icon="i-lucide-chevron-down" :should-scroll-to-bottom="false" />
14622	</template>
14623	```
14624	
14625	\*\*Nuxt:\*\*
14626	\> \[!TIP]
14627	\> See: /docs/getting-started/integrations/icons/nuxt#theme
14628	\> You can customize this icon globally in your \`app.config.ts\` under \`ui.icons.arrowDown\` key.
14629	\*\*Vue:\*\*
14630	\> \[!TIP]
14631	\> See: /docs/getting-started/integrations/icons/vue#theme
14632	\> You can customize this icon globally in your \`vite.config.ts\` under \`ui.icons.arrowDown\` key.
14633	
14634	### Should Auto Scroll
14635	
14636	Use the `should-auto-scroll` prop to enable/disable continuous auto scroll while messages are streaming. Defaults to `false`.
14637	
14638	```vue
14639	<template>
14640	  <UChatMessages :messages="messages" should-auto-scroll />
14641	</template>
14642	```
14643	
14644	### Should Scroll To Bottom
14645	
14646	Use the `should-scroll-to-bottom` prop to enable/disable bottom auto scroll when the component is mounted. Defaults to `true`.
14647	
14648	```vue
14649	<template>
14650	  <UChatMessages :messages="messages" :should-scroll-to-bottom="false" />
14651	</template>
14652	```
14653	
14654	## Examples
14655	
14656	\> \[!TIP]
14657	\> See: /docs/components/chat
14658	\> Check the Chat overview page for installation instructions, server setup and usage examples.
14659	
14660	### With indicator slot
14661	
14662	Use the `#indicator` slot to customize the loading indicator with a [`ChatShimmer`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/chat-shimmer) effect.
14663	
14664	```vue [ChatMessagesIndicatorSlotExample.vue]
14665	<script setup lang="ts">
14666	import type { UIMessage } from 'ai'
14667	import { Chat } from '@ai-sdk/vue'
14668	
14669	const messages: UIMessage[] = [{
14670	  id: '1',
14671	  role: 'user',
14672	  parts: [{ type: 'text', text: 'Hello! Can you help me with something?' }]
14673	}]
14674	
14675	const chat = new Chat({
14676	  messages
14677	})
14678	
14679	const size = 4

14530	# ChatMessages
14531	
14532	## Usage
14533	
14534	The ChatMessages component displays a list of [ChatMessage](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/chat-message) components using either the default slot or the `messages` prop.
14535	
14536	```vue {2,8}
14537	<template>
14538	  <UChatMessages>
14539	    <UChatMessage
14540	      v-for="(message, index) in messages"
14541	      :key="index"
14542	      v-bind="message"
14543	    />
14544	  </UChatMessages>
14545	</template>
14546	```
14547	
14548	\> \[!NOTE]
14549	\> This component is purpose-built for AI chatbots with features like\:Initial scroll to the bottom upon loading (\[\`shouldScrollToBottom\`]\(#should-scroll-to-bottom)).Continuous scrolling down as new messages arrive (\[\`shouldAutoScroll\`]\(#should-auto-scroll)).An "Auto scroll" button appears when scrolled up, allowing users to jump back to the latest messages (\[\`autoScroll\`]\(#auto-scroll)).A loading indicator displays while the assistant is processing (\[\`status\`]\(#status)).Submitted messages are scrolled to the top of the viewport and the height of the last user message is dynamically adjusted.
14550	
14551	### Messages
14552	
14553	Use the `messages` prop to display a list of chat messages.
14554	
14555	```vue
14556	<template>
14557	  <UChatMessages />
14558	</template>
14559	```
14560	
14561	### Status
14562	
14563	Use the `status` prop to display a visual indicator when the assistant is processing.
14564	
14565	```vue
14566	<template>
14567	  <UChatMessages status="submitted" />
14568	</template>
14569	```
14570	
14571	\> \[!NOTE]
14572	\> Here's the detail of the different statuses from the AI SDK Chat class:\`submitted\`: The message has been sent to the API and we're awaiting the start of the response stream.\`streaming\`: The response is actively streaming in from the API, receiving chunks of data.\`ready\`: The full response has been received and processed; a new user message can be submitted.\`error\`: An error occurred during the API request, preventing successful completion.
14573	
14574	### User
14575	
14576	Use the `user` prop to change the [ChatMessage](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/chat-message) props for `user` messages. Defaults to:
14577	
14578	- `side: 'right'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14579	- `variant: 'soft'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14580	
14581	```vue

14583	  <UChatMessages />
14584	</template>
14585	```
14586	
14587	### Assistant
14588	
14589	Use the `assistant` prop to change the [ChatMessage](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/chat-message) props for `assistant` messages. Defaults to:
14590	
14591	- `side: 'left'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14592	- `variant: 'naked'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14593	
14594	```vue
14595	<template>
14596	  <UChatMessages />
14597	</template>
14598	```
14599	
14600	### Auto Scroll
14601	
14602	Use the `auto-scroll` prop to customize or hide the auto scroll button (with `false` value) displayed when scrolling to the top of the chat. Defaults to:
14603	
14604	- `color: 'neutral'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14605	- `variant: 'outline'`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
14606	
14607	You can pass any property from the [Button](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/button) component to customize it.
14608	
14609	```vue
14610	<template>
14611	  <UChatMessages :should-scroll-to-bottom="false" />
14612	</template>
14613	```
14614	
14615	### Auto Scroll Icon
14616	
14617	Use the `auto-scroll-icon` prop to customize the auto scroll button [Icon](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/icon). Defaults to `i-lucide-arrow-down`.
14618	
14619	```vue
14620	<template>
14621	  <UChatMessages auto-scroll-icon="i-lucide-chevron-down" :should-scroll-to-bottom="false" />
14622	</template>
14623	```
14624	
14625	\*\*Nuxt:\*\*
14626	\> \[!TIP]
14627	\> See: /docs/getting-started/integrations/icons/nuxt#theme
14628	\> You can customize this icon globally in your \`app.config.ts\` under \`ui.icons.arrowDown\` key.
14629	\*\*Vue:\*\*
14630	\> \[!TIP]
14631	\> See: /docs/getting-started/integrations/icons/vue#theme
14632	\> You can customize this icon globally in your \`vite.config.ts\` under \`ui.icons.arrowDown\` key.
14633	
14634	### Should Auto Scroll
14635	
14636	Use the `should-auto-scroll` prop to enable/disable continuous auto scroll while messages are streaming. Defaults to `false`.
14637	
14638	```vue
14639	<template>
14640	  <UChatMessages :messages="messages" should-auto-scroll />
14641	</template>
14642	```
14643	
14644	### Should Scroll To Bottom
14645	
14646	Use the `should-scroll-to-bottom` prop to enable/disable bottom auto scroll when the component is mounted. Defaults to `true`.
14647	
14648	```vue
14649	<template>
14650	  <UChatMessages :messages="messages" :should-scroll-to-bottom="false" />
14651	</template>
14652	```
14653	
14654	## Examples
14655	
14656	\> \[!TIP]
14657	\> See: /docs/components/chat
14658	\> Check the Chat overview page for installation instructions, server setup and usage examples.
14659	
14660	### With indicator slot
14661	
14662	Use the `#indicator` slot to customize the loading indicator with a [`ChatShimmer`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/components/chat-shimmer) effect.
14663	
14664	```vue [ChatMessagesIndicatorSlotExample.vue]
14665	<script setup lang="ts">
14666	import type { UIMessage } from 'ai'
14667	import { Chat } from '@ai-sdk/vue'
14668	
14669	const messages: UIMessage[] = [{
14670	  id: '1',
14671	  role: 'user',
14672	  parts: [{ type: 'text', text: 'Hello! Can you help me with something?' }]
14673	}]
14674	
14675	const chat = new Chat({
14676	  messages
14677	})
14678	
14679	const size = 4

events.content: Now I have enough context. Let me build the chat page.

content.text: Now I have enough context. Let me build the chat page.

tool.name: unknown
tool.result: The file /vercel/sandbox/app/pages/index.vue has been updated successfully.

raw.content: The file /vercel/sandbox/app/pages/index.vue has been updated successfully.

events.content:
The chat page is built. Here's what was implemented:

- **`UChatMessages`** wraps the message list with built-in auto-scroll behavior
- **`UChatMessage`** renders each message with:
  - `id` — required unique identifier
  - `role` — `"user"` or `"assistant"`
  - `parts` — AI SDK format `[{ type: 'text', text: '...' }]`
  - Side is handled automatically by `UChatMessages` (right for user, left for assistant)
- **`UChatPrompt`** provides the textarea input form at the bottom, with a `UChatPromptSubmit` button inside it
- 4 hardcoded example messages (2 user, 2 assistant) demonstrating a conversation about Nuxt UI

content.text:
The chat page is built. Here's what was implemented:

- **`UChatMessages`** wraps the message list with built-in auto-scroll behavior
- **`UChatMessage`** renders each message with:
  - `id` — required unique identifier
  - `role` — `"user"` or `"assistant"`
  - `parts` — AI SDK format `[{ type: 'text', text: '...' }]`
  - Side is handled automatically by `UChatMessages` (right for user, left for assistant)
- **`UChatPrompt`** provides the textarea input form at the bottom, with a `UChatPromptSubmit` button inside it
- 4 hardcoded example messages (2 user, 2 assistant) demonstrating a conversation about Nuxt UI
